meta_title: Impact Doors in North Lauderdale, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in North Lauderdale, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 100% in-house, licensed & insured. Free estimates — from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Doors Built for North Lauderdale Homes — Protection You Can Count On Every Season
intro:
North Lauderdale may sit roughly eight miles from the Atlantic coastline, but any longtime resident of Ramblewood or North Lauderdale Village will tell you that distance from the water offers little comfort when a major hurricane rolls through Broward County. With nine named storms making their mark on this community since 1990 — including the catastrophic Hurricane Andrew in 1992, the record-breaking Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and the wide-reaching Hurricane Irma in 2017 — North Lauderdale homeowners know firsthand that powerful winds don't stop at the shoreline. The city sits in a 170 mph wind design zone, a designation under Florida Building Code that demands serious, certified hurricane protection for every opening in your home, including all entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors.

For the nearly half of North Lauderdale homes built before 1994 — many of them in established neighborhoods around Hampton Pines Park and along the corridors that connect the city to Lauderdale Lakes, Tamarac, and Margate — the original doors were simply not engineered with modern storm standards in mind. Older wooden entry doors and hollow-core designs can fail under wind pressure well below 100 mph, let alone the sustained gusts that a Category 2 or stronger storm can deliver to inland Broward communities. Upgrading to certified impact doors is not just a smart home improvement decision; in many cases, it is a code-required one when undertaking permitted renovations. With the average North Lauderdale home valued at approximately $290,000, protecting that investment with impact-rated doors is one of the highest-return upgrades a homeowner can make.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties as "The Kings of Service" — and nowhere is that service more meaningful than in working-class, family-oriented communities like North Lauderdale where homeowners want honest advice, quality products, and installations done right the first time. We install ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact door systems exclusively through our own in-house crews, meaning no subcontractors, no miscommunication, and no shortcuts. From the moment we pull your permit through the City of North Lauderdale Building Division to the day your new impact door is inspected and approved, we stand beside you every step of the way.

section_why.h2: Why North Lauderdale Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
When you're investing in hurricane protection for your home in North Lauderdale, choosing the right contractor matters just as much as choosing the right product. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings more than 60 years of combined industry experience to every job we take on in Broward County. Our team understands the unique construction styles common to North Lauderdale's housing stock — from the single-story CBS (concrete block and stucco) homes built in the 1970s and 1980s near Ramblewood to the slightly newer townhome communities closer to North Lauderdale City Hall. Every home presents its own challenges, and our technicians are trained to handle all of them.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our commitment to 100% in-house installation. We do not use subcontractors — ever. When you schedule an installation with us, the same licensed, experienced crew that arrives on day one will be the same team completing your project. This approach ensures consistency, accountability, and quality control that simply cannot be guaranteed when work is handed off to third parties. Our crews handle everything from precise door frame preparation and flashing to final caulking, hardware installation, and inspection coordination with the City of North Lauderdale Building Division. You won't be left managing the process yourself.

Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and our track record of more than 500 completed homes across South Florida reflect what we believe in: doing the job correctly, treating homeowners with respect, and standing behind our work long after the installation is complete. For lower-middle income households in North Lauderdale where every dollar counts, we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available — because quality storm protection shouldn't be out of reach for any family. We also help homeowners navigate the insurance and wind mitigation process to maximize their savings after installation, adding even more long-term value to the investment.

We are fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, and our familiarity with Broward County permitting processes — including the specific requirements enforced by the City of North Lauderdale Building Division — means your installation will be fully code-compliant and inspection-ready without delays or complications. When neighboring cities like Tamarac and Margate residents ask their friends for contractor referrals, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is consistently the name that comes up.

section_history.h2: Storm History Has Shaped How North Lauderdale Thinks About Door Protection
section_history.body:
North Lauderdale's storm history reads like a reminder that inland Broward County communities are not insulated from hurricane devastation. Hurricane Andrew in August 1992 remains the defining storm for an entire generation of South Florida homeowners. While the most catastrophic damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade, the storm's outer bands and preceding wind field caused significant structural failures across Broward County, exposing how unprepared the existing housing stock really was. Doors — especially entry doors with single-point latches and hollow cores — failed in large numbers, allowing wind-driven rain to penetrate homes and cause flooding and structural damage that far exceeded the cost of proper protection.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 brought a different but equally painful lesson. Wilma crossed the Florida peninsula as a powerful Category 3 storm and tracked directly through Broward County, producing sustained winds that caused widespread damage across North Lauderdale neighborhoods. Sliding glass doors that had survived prior storms finally gave way under Wilma's prolonged wind loading, and the storm surge and rain intrusion that followed left many families dealing with mold, interior damage, and insurance disputes for months afterward. Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017, which, though weakened before landfall, still produced tropical storm to low-hurricane force winds across the county and reminded homeowners that every storm season carries real risk.

With nine storms impacting the area since 1990, North Lauderdale's storm history is not an abstract statistic — it is the lived experience of families in North Lauderdale Village, Ramblewood, and every block in between. Impact doors are the single most effective barrier between your family and the destructive combination of wind pressure and wind-driven rain that storms consistently deliver to this community. Every year without adequate door protection is a year of unnecessary risk.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in North Lauderdale and Broward County
section_code.body:
North Lauderdale falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition, which establishes specific requirements for all exterior openings based on the city's wind design speed. The city is designated as a 170 mph wind zone, which means all new or replacement impact doors must be tested and certified to withstand that level of wind pressure, as well as the impact of wind-borne debris. While North Lauderdale is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved for Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the coast — the city still requires impact-rated products that carry valid Florida Product Approval numbers and meet the Large Missile Impact Test (ASTM E1886/E1996) standards.

All impact door installations in North Lauderdale require a permit through the City of North Lauderdale Building Division, located at North Lauderdale City Hall. Unpermitted work — regardless of the product used — can create serious complications when you attempt to sell your home, file an insurance claim, or refinance your mortgage. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application submission to scheduling the required inspections and obtaining the final certificate of completion. Our team's deep familiarity with the City of North Lauderdale Building Division's workflows means your project moves through the process efficiently.

For homes built before 1994 — which make up approximately 48% of North Lauderdale's housing stock — there may be additional considerations related to existing frame conditions, rough opening dimensions, and structural attachments that must be addressed to bring the installation into full code compliance. Our pre-installation assessments identify these issues before work begins, ensuring that your new impact doors are not just installed, but installed correctly and to the full letter of current Florida Building Code requirements.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Engineered for North Lauderdale Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted names in the impact door industry: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands carry valid Florida Product Approval certifications and are engineered specifically for the demanding wind and debris conditions found in South Florida communities like North Lauderdale. Whether you need a fortified entry door for the front of your CBS home, a new sliding glass door for your back patio, or elegant French doors that don't sacrifice security for style, our ESW and CWI product lines offer solutions designed to perform year after year in Broward County's climate.

ESW impact entry doors feature laminated glass with a shatter-resistant interlayer bonded to heavy-duty aluminum or fiberglass frames, delivering both impact resistance and superior thermal performance. Their multi-point locking systems engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously — a critical feature that dramatically improves resistance to wind-pressure-induced door failure compared to standard single-bolt locks. For sliding glass doors and French doors, ESW's track systems are engineered to maintain weathertight seals even under significant deflection, preventing the water intrusion that has been a primary source of storm-related home damage in North Lauderdale's storm history.

CWI products complement our ESW line with additional options in terms of size configurations, glass tint and opacity choices, and frame color selections — giving North Lauderdale homeowners the flexibility to upgrade their storm protection without compromising the curb appeal of their homes. Both brands are backed by manufacturer warranties and are installed by our in-house crews using manufacturer-approved methods, ensuring that warranty coverage remains fully intact. When you combine certified impact-rated hardware with our experienced installation team, you get a door system that protects your home, enhances its appearance, and adds meaningful value to your property.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in North Lauderdale — And How to Finance Them Affordably
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in North Lauderdale varies depending on the type, size, and number of doors being replaced, as well as any site-specific conditions related to your home's existing frame or rough opening. For a typical single-family home in North Lauderdale with an average value around $290,000, a single impact entry door installation might range from approximately $1,500 to $3,500 fully installed, while sliding glass door replacements and French door systems typically range from $2,500 to $6,000 or more depending on the size of the opening and glass specifications. Whole-home door packages — covering all exterior doors — will vary accordingly but often unlock better per-unit pricing.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed to make storm protection accessible for North Lauderdale families across income levels. Our financing programs start at just $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. We work with multiple lending partners to find the right payment structure for your budget, and our team will walk you through every option clearly and honestly — no pressure, no hidden fees. For a home in North Lauderdale, investing in impact doors through a manageable monthly payment is far more practical than facing a five-figure repair bill after a storm event.

Beyond the upfront cost, the return on investment for impact doors in North Lauderdale is compelling. Homeowners typically see measurable reductions in their annual homeowners insurance premiums following a wind mitigation inspection that credits their new impact-rated openings. Energy savings from improved thermal insulation are an additional ongoing benefit, as impact-rated glass significantly reduces solar heat gain — a meaningful advantage in Broward County's year-round warm climate. Combined with the increased resale value that certified hurricane protection adds to a North Lauderdale home, the long-term ROI of impact door installation consistently justifies the upfront investment.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for North Lauderdale Homeowners After Impact Door Installation
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact doors in North Lauderdale is the potential reduction in your homeowners insurance premium. Florida's insurance market has grown increasingly difficult for homeowners across Broward County, with carriers tightening underwriting standards and raising rates in response to years of storm losses and reinsurance cost increases. However, homes with certified impact-resistant openings — including impact doors — qualify for wind mitigation credits that can produce meaningful premium discounts, often ranging from 15% to 45% depending on the extent of the protection and your current carrier's rating methodology.

After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your impact door installation, we recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed Florida inspector. The inspector evaluates your home's opening protections and produces a wind mitigation report that you submit to your insurance carrier. Major carriers active in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance Corporation (the state-backed insurer of last resort), as well as private carriers that operate in the North Lauderdale market — use this report to apply credits under Florida's Wind Mitigation Credits law, which requires insurers to offer these discounts on qualifying homes. The savings accumulate every year, further reducing the effective cost of your door investment over time.

For North Lauderdale homeowners whose homes were built before the post-Andrew building code revisions of 1994, the insurance benefit of upgrading all exterior openings to impact-rated products can be particularly significant. Older homes without opening protection often carry higher base wind premiums, meaning the relative discount from adding certified impact doors may be larger than what newer-construction homeowners experience. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team can help you understand how to document your installation properly for the wind mitigation process and connect you with inspection resources in the Broward County area to ensure you capture every dollar of savings available to you.

faqs.q: Are impact doors required by law for homes in North Lauderdale, or are they optional?
faqs.a: While existing North Lauderdale homes are not required to retrofit impact doors purely by virtue of age, any new door installation completed under a permit must meet current Florida Building Code standards for the city's 170 mph wind zone. This effectively requires impact-rated products for all permitted replacements. Additionally, when undertaking substantial renovations, code compliance for all exterior openings may be triggered. We recommend consulting with our team or the City of North Lauderdale Building Division for guidance specific to your project.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in North Lauderdale, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in North Lauderdale typically range from around $1,500 to $6,000 or more per door depending on type, size, and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. Our team will provide a detailed, no-obligation estimate for your specific home and help you find a payment plan that fits your budget without compromising on quality or code compliance.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace doors with impact doors in North Lauderdale?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door installations in North Lauderdale require a permit issued by the City of North Lauderdale Building Division. Unpermitted work can create issues with insurance claims, home resale, and mortgage refinancing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the full permitting process on your behalf — from application to final inspection — ensuring your installation is code-compliant, properly documented, and fully approved by local authorities without any burden placed on you.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical North Lauderdale home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in North Lauderdale, a single impact door installation can be completed in one day. Multi-door projects — such as replacing an entry door, a sliding glass door, and French doors simultaneously — typically take one to two days of installation work. The permitting process, which runs parallel to product ordering, usually takes two to four weeks depending on current Building Division workload. Our team coordinates everything so that by the time your doors arrive, your permit is ready and your installation can proceed without delay.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance after installing impact doors in North Lauderdale?
faqs.a: North Lauderdale homeowners who install certified impact-rated doors and complete a wind mitigation inspection can typically expect insurance premium reductions ranging from 15% to 45%, depending on the extent of opening protection and their carrier's credit schedule. For a home in North Lauderdale with average insurance premiums in the Broward County market, this can translate to hundreds of dollars in annual savings. Over the lifetime of the doors, these cumulative savings contribute significantly to the overall return on your installation investment.

cta_body: The next hurricane season won't wait, and neither should your home's protection. Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing now for a no-obligation consultation and discover how affordable certified impact door installation can be for your North Lauderdale home.
